rulings.Case Title: MONICA HUJAZI, AN INDIVIDUIAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE VS. SYDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Sidney A. Luscutoff And Luscutoff, Lendormy And Associates For Terminating Sanctions For Disobedience Of Discovery Orders
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 1, Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Sidney A. Luscutoff And Luscutoff, Lendormy And Associates For Terminating Sanctions For Disobedience Of Discovery Orders. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed Motion is GRANTED. Defendants are ordered to prepare a proposed order that lists out each of the prior orders that defendants contend plaintiffs disobeyed. Defendant shall email the proposed order in Word format to the Judge Pro Tem via email before 5pm today. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO MARIN COUNTYY ** GOLDEN GATE REGIONAL CENTER VS. OFFICE OF ADMINISTRATIVE HEARINGS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 10, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST MARIN COUNTY PUBLIC GUARDIAN ON BEHALF OF ERIKA S. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue. Real Party in Interest Erika S.'s motion to transfer venue to Marin County is granted. This case will be transferred to Marin County Superior Court and all costs of transfer must be paid by petitioner Golden Gate Regional Center. Code of Civil Procedure 393(b) is not applicable to this case because respondents Office of Administrative Hearings and Department of Developmental Services are nominal parties and this writ petition is truly against Erika, not OAH or DDS. (Sutter Union High School District v. Superior Court (1983) 140 Cal. App. 3d. 795, 798 (the public agency whose decision was sought to be set aside by a writ of mandate petition "is a nominal party whose residence is immaterial for venue purposes"); see also Hayes v. California Department of Development Services (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 1523 (on a writ petition seeking to set aside a decision of the OAH, the OAH is not a necessary or indispensable party and the failure to name it as a respondent within limitations period did not warrant dismissal of the writ petition).) Because Erika is neither a public officer nor did she assume the role of a public officer, by its own terms, section 393(b) is not applicable, which makes Code of Civil Procedure 395(a) the applicable venue statute. Per section 395(a), venue is proper only in Marin County where Erika resides. Erika's request that she be awarded fees and costs per Code of Civil Procedure 396(b) is denied because GGRC's choice of venue in San Francisco was not made in bad faith due to this case have several attributes related to San Francisco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Erika is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER MCDONALD ET AL VS. 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, MATTHIAS SPANLE, MARK SHER, PAMELA YOON'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement. Defendants' motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted in substantial part. The $50,000 on deposit with the court will be released to defendant 830 Lake Street Homeowner's Association, but may be used only to pay for the services of an "independent board" or an "independent property manager." The court is aware that there is no independent board and may never be one. The court defines "independent property manager" as a person or company in the business of managing condominium properties in San Francisco and has no business or personal connection with any of the owners of the units prior to the person or company's hiring or the date of the settlement agreement, whichever is later. This interpretation of the settlement agreement comports with both the language of the agreement and the intent of the parties. Defendants' request for fees and costs is denied. Paragraph 8.2 of the settlement agreement does not provide for prevailing party fees in a dispute about the $50,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN RAFFI VS. EXTRA SPACE STORAGE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Discovery Referee And Request For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTS EXTRA SPACE STORAGE, INC., EXTRA SPACE MANAGEMENT, INC., STORAGE TREASURES LLC'S Motion To Appoint Discovery Referee And Request For A Protective Order. Defendants' motion to appoint discovery referee and for a protective order is granted in substantial part. The court will appoint a discovery referee due to the extraordinary number of discovery disputes in this case and all fees of the discovery referee will be paid by defendants per their agreement to do so. The discovery referee is empowered to: 1) resolve all discovery disputes between the parties; 2) require the submission to him or her of discovery plans for his/her approval; 3) preclude any discovery not propounded or taken per a pre-approved discovery plan. All discovery and discovery motions are stayed pending the appointment of the discovery referee and an initial hearing with the discovery referee. If the parties cannot agree on the person to serve as their discovery referee, per Code of Civil Procedure 640(b), no later than May 15, 2018, each side shall submit the names of up to three people whom he or they believe are qualified to serve as a discovery referee and the hearing on this motion will be continued to May 17, 2018 for the court to announce which of the nominees will be selected as the parties' discovery referee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SMITH VS. YOUSEF WALLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANTS HILDA CHAN, ASIAN PACIFIC ISLANDER LEGAL OUTREACH'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Hilda Chan and Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the sixth through tenth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by the plaintiff suing under the fictitious name of John Smith is granted as to the sixth through eighth causes of action and denied as to the ninth and tenth causes of action. Plaintiff concedes that at the trial court level his seventh cause of action for malicious prosecution should be stricken per the anti-SLAPP statute. Ms. Chan and API satisfied their first prong burden of showing that the sixth cause of action for defamation and the eighth cause of action for abuse of process arise from activity protected by Code of Civil Procedure 425.16(e)(1) and/or (2) as written or oral statements made before an executive or judicial proceeding and/or in connection with an issue under consideration or review by an executive or judicial body. Plaintiff did not satisfy his second prong burden of showing that his defamation and abuse of process claims have the required minimal merit because those claims are barred by the litigation privilege. Ms. Chan and API did not satisfy their first prong burden of showing that the ninth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress arose from protected activity because the gravamen of that cause of action against Ms. Chan and API is their alleged participation in the unconsented distribution of a sensitive video that infringed on plaintiff's protected privacy rights. Ms. Chan and API did not satisfy their first prong burden of showing that the tenth cause of action for negligence arose from protected activity because that cause of action is devoid of any factual allegations. (Central Valley Hospitalists v. Dignity Health (2018) 19 Cal. App. 5th 203). As to that cause of action, moving defendants' remedy is to file a demurrer, not an anti-SLAPP motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

Plaintiff did not satisfy his second prong burden of showing that his defamation and abuse of process claims have the required minimal merit because those claims are barred by the litigation privilege. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order To Seal Unredacted Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint And Its Attached Exhibit A Under Cal. Rules Of Court, Rule 2.551; Sanctions Under Ccp 177.5; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND'S Motion For Order To Seal Unredacted Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint and for Sanctions Under Ccp 177.5. Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's motion for order to seal unredacted portions of plaintiff's complaint and for sanctions is continued to May 29, 2018 to give State Fund an opportunity to substantively respond to the argument in plaintiffs' opposition memorandum that sealing of the settlement agreement is contrary to applicable law. While the court agrees with State Fund that the better course was for plaintiffs to file a motion seeking to unseal the settlement agreement rather than filing the settlement agreement as an exhibit to the complaint in a manner that makes the agreement available to the public, due to the important interest of making court files available to the public where warranted, the proper course now is for the court to address the substantive issue of whether sealing of the settlement agreement comports with California law. State Fund may file supplemental reply papers limited to that issue no later than May 21, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D ET AL VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 9, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's motion for order striking the complaint is denied and its alternative motion for stay of this case pending the conclusion of the appeal of the judgment dated April 20, 2017 in case number 492766 is granted. There is no basis to strike the complaint. However, because all of the claims in the complaint are based on the terms and interpretation of the parties' settlement which is the gravamen of the appeal, per Code of Civil Procedure 916, this court currently lacks jurisdiction over the subject matter of those claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
